_note: Hand-authored (Tier R) input for tools/build_breeding.py --emit-relocation (ROADMAP Phase 2B / B3). These SPECIAL recipes are APPENDED after the 825 vanilla/patched entries inside the relocated bank $69 table, proving the table can grow past index 824 (capacity 1x-2x). The bank $69 scanner walks to the $FF terminator, so appended entries are scanned with normal first-match-wins precedence. Real breeding content belongs in B5; this file exists only to exercise the >824 capacity.
_consumed_by: tools/build_breeding.py (read by load_extra_recipes); emitted into patches/bank_069.asm
_format: each recipe = {p1, p2, min_plus, result, plus_mod}; p1=Pedigree matcher ($DA6F/$DA73), p2=Mate matcher ($DA70/$DA74); a species ID 0-220 OR a family code $F0-$FA; result is a species ID 0-220 (written to $DA71); ids are decimal here
_capacity: base 825 + appended must not exceed SPECIAL_CAPACITY_MAX (1650) and must fit bank $69
_shadow_note: An appended cross only fires if NO base entry (0-824) matches the same parents first. The tool's --emit-relocation self-check FAILS on a shadowed (dead) appended recipe. NOTE: the FORWARD order MadCat(Pedigree) x BattleRex(Mate) is already a VANILLA recipe (base entry 187 -> Yeti $3B), so it is SHADOWED and cannot demonstrate >824 capacity. The REVERSE order below (BattleRex Pedigree x MadCat Mate) is unshadowed and free.

recipes._comment: B3 capacity proof: BattleRex(Pedigree,$2A=42) x MadCat(Mate,$44=68) -> DracoLord($C8=200). UNSHADOWED by all 825 base entries, so DracoLord can ONLY come from this >824 entry. min_plus 0 = fires at any plus value. TEST: breed with BattleRex as the Pedigree (first/base parent) and MadCat as the Mate; opposite genders, Lv10+.
